ops.title: In repo-bootstrap repos (colocated jj), raw git commit/push silently no-ops the main ref; use jj or ccx vcs ship
ops.body:
Repos scaffolded by `/repo-bootstrap:repo-bootstrap` are **colocated jj repos** (`.jj/` present, `jj git init --git-repo .`). Git sits in **detached HEAD** because jj owns the working copy — so `git commit` advances detached HEAD but does **not** move the `main` branch ref, and `git push origin main` then pushes the *stale* `main` (a silent no-op that still prints "pushed"). Symptom seen on experiment-at-home: several `git commit` + `git push` cycles all reported success while `origin/main` stayed pinned at an old commit; `gh api .../commits/main` proved the pushes never landed.

**Why:** `git log --oneline main` shows the branch ref, which lags; `jj log` shows the real chain (working copy `@` on top of the loose commits). The loose commits exist in jj but no bookmark points the git `main` ref at them.

**How to apply:** On these repos, commit and push with **jj** or **`ccx vcs ship`** (jj-aware), not raw git. To recover after raw-git commits stranded the branch:
1. `jj bookmark set main -r <change-id-of-real-tip>` (find it in `jj log`; the tip is usually the parent of the empty `@` working-copy commit).
2. First time only: `jj bookmark track main@origin` (else push errors "Non-tracking remote bookmark main@origin exists").
3. `jj git push --bookmark main`.
4. Verify with `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/commits/main -q '.sha'` — never trust the local `git push` "pushed" line alone.

Note: `ccx vcs ship` reported "pushed main → origin · CI no-run" on this repo but did **not** actually move the ref either in one case — always verify origin after ship on a fresh colocated repo. Related: [[cc-steer-rename-decisions]].
